{"key_event":"Rights-based devolution after poll tax backlash","reasoning":"Civil society recovered some checking power through the poll tax revolt, media/NGO campaigning, rights politics, devolution pressure, and the Northern Ireland peace process, despite weakened trade unions and older working-class institutions. State capacity remained very high, with stronger regulatory, fiscal, and technocratic tools, but Westminster/executive discretion was modestly constrained by devolution, European/legal rights, Bank of England independence, and market credibility shocks.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":9.4,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":9.1}
